disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: French passport holders currently receive a 60-day visa exemption on arrival. Extendable once at any immigration office for 30 additional days. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond entry date.
visa.verify_note: Thailand has revised exemption durations multiple times. Confirm current policy at thaiembassy.fr or official Thai immigration site before travel.

insurance.notes: Not legally required but strongly recommended. Ensure your policy covers: motorbike riding (often excluded by default), medical evacuation, adventure activities. Hospital costs for serious incidents can reach €20,000+.

regions.notes: Hot season Mar–May. Rainy season Jun–Oct.
Smoke season Mar–Apr due to burning. Best weather Nov–Feb.
Opposite rainy season to the rest of the country.
Monsoon May–Oct can make seas rough and beaches closed.

budget.verify_note: Rates are approximate. Check xe.com before travel.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed, street food 3x, local transport, 1 beer

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Private guesthouse, mix of restaurants, Grab rides, activities

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation — download offline maps before arriving

useful_apps.name: Airalo
useful_apps.use: eSIM data — buy before you land

useful_apps.name: LINE
useful_apps.use: Main local messaging app — useful for guesthouses & tours

transport: Use Grab for all city rides — always cheaper and safer than hailing tuk-tuks or taxis without a meter.
Bangkok has excellent BTS Skytrain and MRT metro — buy a Rabbit Card for convenience.
Overnight trains (State Railway of Thailand) are cheap and scenic — book at railway.co.th.
For island hopping, use 12Go Asia to compare ferry + bus combo tickets.
Domestic flights are affordable: AirAsia and Thai Lion Air cover most routes under ฿1,000 if booked early.
Motorbike rental is common outside cities but carry your international driving licence (motorbike category) and always wear a helmet.

scams.name: Tuk-tuk 'temple tour'
scams.description: A friendly driver offers to take you to temples for almost free, then diverts to gem shops, tailor shops or travel agencies where they earn commission. Refuse politely and use Grab.

scams.name: Airport taxi 'hotel is closed'
scams.description: Taxi drivers at Suvarnabhumi claim your hotel is closed or flooded and offer to take you to a 'better' one. Ignore — call your hotel directly.

scams.name: Gem scam
scams.description: A well-dressed stranger says today is a special government sale and invites you to a jewellery shop. The gems are worthless. This is a classic, persistent Bangkok scam.

scams.name: Jet ski damage claim
scams.description: Phuket and Pattaya: rental operators claim pre-existing damage on returned jet skis. Always photograph the vehicle thoroughly before riding.

scams.name: Ping-pong show tout
scams.description: Touted 'free entry' shows end with a ฿2,000+ bill. Avoid entirely.

phrases.meaning: Hello (krap = male speaker, ka = female speaker)
I don't want it (politely declines touts)

french_embassy_bangkok.address: 35 Soi 36 Charoen Krung Road, Bangrak, Bangkok

emergency.notes: Tourist Police (1155) have English-speaking officers and handle tourist-related incidents. Use before regular police for theft, scams, etc.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond your intended departure date
Check current visa exemption duration at thaiembassy.fr
Print or save a return/onward ticket — may be checked at immigration
Notify your bank of travel dates to avoid card blocks
Download Google Maps offline for Bangkok and target regions
Buy Airalo eSIM or arrange SIM before / on arrival
Bring some EUR to exchange on arrival (airports have fair rates at SuperRich booths)
Save Thai emergency numbers in your phone
Check baggage rules for domestic flights if island hopping
Pack light — 35L backpack is sufficient for most itineraries
